abstract: The object for defining the text style, such as font family, size, and color, that you can apply to ranges of text.
overview:
## Discussion

A `TextStyle` object defines a style that you can apply to ranges of text. Include a property, with a name that you define and a `TextStyle` object for the value, in the [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/ArticleDocument/textStyles-data.dictionary] object. Then,  you can use that property’s name string together with HTML, Markdown, or [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/InlineTextStyle] to apply the style to a range of text.

If you’re using Markdown syntax to style your text, see the [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/using-markdown-with-apple-news-format#Inline-Text-Style] section in [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/using-markdown-with-apple-news-format]. If you’re using HTML, see [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/using-html-with-apple-news-format].

You can use this object in [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/ArticleDocument/textStyles-data.dictionary].

### Example
rawMarkdown:
--- source: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/TextStyle crawled: 2026-03-10T14:19:12Z --- # TextStyle **Object** The object for defining the text style, such as font family, size, and color, that you can apply to ranges of text. ## Declaration ```swift object TextStyle ``` ## Discussion A `TextStyle` object defines a style that you can apply to ranges of text. Include a property, with a name that you define and a `TextStyle` object for the value, in the [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/ArticleDocument/textStyles-data.dictionary] object. Then, you can use that property’s name string together with HTML, Markdown, or [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/InlineTextStyle] to apply the style to a range of text. If you’re using Markdown syntax to style your text, see the [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/using-markdown-with-apple-news-format#Inline-Text-Style] section in [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/using-markdown-with-apple-news-format]. If you’re using HTML, see [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/using-html-with-apple-news-format]. You can use this object in [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/ArticleDocument/textStyles-data.dictionary]. ### Example ```json { "textStyles": { "default-tag-abbr": { "textColor": "#34af15" } }, "components": [ { "role": "body", "format": "html", "text": "

The WWF is an international wildlife fund.

" } ] } ``` ## Related Documentation - **Adding Color to Text Ranges**: Create text in color by using HTML to refer to TextStyle objects. ## Text Styles - **Defining and Applying Text Styles**: Define and apply custom, default, and inline text styles, or use HTML tags or Markdown syntax to style your text. - **Applying Apple News Format Fonts**: Choose a font family for Apple News Format that’s supported in iOS, iPadOS, and macOS. - **ComponentTextStyle**: The object for defining the style for a text component, including spacing, alignment, and drop caps. - **DropCapStyle**: The object for defining the drop cap text style to use in the first paragraph in a text component. - **ListItemStyle**: The object for defining the style for bulleted or numbered lists in an article. - **InlineTextStyle**: The object for applying text styling when not using HTML or Markdown formatting. ## Inherited By - ComponentTextStyle - ConditionalComponentTextStyle - ConditionalTextStyle
